item.title: v1.4.2
item.description:
• Photo-real rock. Boulder's surface now has multi-octave value noise, procedural cracks following a 2D wave field, vertical weathering streaks, moss/lichen flecks in the shaded quadrant, ambient occlusion at the edges, and a cast shadow on the ground.
  • Stage vignette. The rock now sits in a subtle inset radial gradient — feels like the boulder is in a space, not floating.
  • Refined popover header. Tier name + "N px to next tier" subtitle, dedicated momentum capsule (green glow dot, label, multiplier), and the pixel count as a quieter monospace.

item.title: v1.4.1
item.description:
• Critical crash fix. BoulderShape.computeCells trapped on a signed-int overflow the first time pixels were emitted. Every focus session was dying within seconds. Hash math now uses safe UInt32 truncation.
  • Slower, more deliberate growth. 1 pixel per 5 minutes at 1× momentum (was 1 per 12 seconds). Mountain tier is now a real 6-month commitment, not a weekend grind. Momentum tier multiplier still ramps 1× → 3× during long sessions.
  • App blocker now pushes back. Switching to a blocked app during focus now hides it, brings Boulder forward, opens the popover, and crumbles pixels. macOS won't grant indie apps the FamilyControls entitlement to true-block apps — this is the closest we can get.
  • Menubar boulder fix. Logo now renders thread-safely via NSImage drawingHandler with pre-resolved colors. No more sub-pixel speck.
  • Notifications in foreground. Crumble banners now show even while Boulder is frontmost.

item.title: v1.4.0
item.description:
• Real-looking rock. Pixels now snap to a dense dome silhouette — every cell is integer-grid-aligned, packed edge-to-edge, lit from above with a 20-shade granite gradient and subtle vein inclusions. No more scattered rubble.
  • Connected silhouette. The boulder is a single coherent mass at every pixel count, growing wider and taller in proportion as you focus.
  • Per-tag rock tints. Tags now produce 20 smoothly-interpolated shades capped at low saturation — Granite/Slate/Basalt/Jade/etc. each read as tinted stone, never as a vivid hue.
  • Automatic migration. Existing rocks are reshaped on first launch — tag and session attribution preserved, only the (x,y,shade) positions update.
  • Pixel-perfect rendering. Renderer rounds baseline + cell size to integer pixels to kill sub-pixel anti-aliasing seams.

item.title: v1.3.0
item.description:
• You make your own tags. First launch starts empty; you create the categories that matter to YOU with a name, emoji, color, and description.
  • Commitment sessions. Pre-select 15m / 25m / 45m / 1h / 90m / Open. Committed sessions count DOWN and earn a small bonus on completion. Giving up early chips 25% of session pixels off Boulder.
  • Session descriptions stamped onto every pixel you earn. Click a pixel later → see what you were doing.
  • Pixel inspector — tap your rock to surface the session that grew the pixel cluster you tapped (tag, description, date, gave-up flag).
  • Tag library in Settings → Tags. Hue slider for color, palette preview, edit/delete.
  • Force-quit-safe commitment. Quitting Boulder mid-commitment counts as a give-up — no backdoor escape.
  • Honest note: macOS won't let an indie app system-lock you out of your computer. Boulder builds commitment through pixel stakes instead.

item.title: v1.2.0
item.description:
• Momentum tiers. The longer you focus in one session, the more pixels per minute you earn. 1× warming up, ramps to 3× after an hour of continuous focus. Stop = reset. No punishment for breaks — just a reward for staying deep.
  • You always see your rock. Even with zero pixels, the popover shows a starter pebble silhouette. Auto-scales so a 5-pixel Boulder and a 5000-pixel Mountain both feel substantial.
  • Real app icon. Procedurally generated pixel boulder on a night-sky backdrop. Visible in Finder, the DMG, the About panel.
  • Momentum readout. "Rolling × 1.5×" in the popover header during focus sessions.

item.title: v1.1.0
item.description:
• Boulder shows up in your menubar. The icon is the actual rock, growing pixel by pixel as you focus — no more emoji placeholder.
  • App blocking. Pick the apps that break your focus (Slack, Twitter, Discord). Switching to one during a focus session chips 3 pixels off Boulder and posts a notification. 8-second cooldown so a single mis-tab doesn't punish you twice.
  • Settings actually work. Cmd+, opens a real settings window with launch-at-login, blocked apps, and an About tab.
  • Crumble flash. Boulder shakes and shows a red -3px counter when your focus breaks.
  • Popover polish. Live "Focusing" indicator, blocked-apps strip, cleaner tier label.

item.title: v1.0.0
item.description:
• Boulder is born. A pet rock for your focus that can't die — it just grows, one pixel at a time, every minute you focus.
  • Five focus types — code, write, read, audio, design — each stamps a different texture into your Boulder.
  • Mountain Range gallery for retired Boulders. Release a Mountain-sized Boulder into the sea; it joins your skyline.
  • Menubar only. No Dock icon, no nagging, no streak guilt. Truly chill.
